title: Clear Turkish Vegetable Stir Fry
description: A light and refreshing Turkish dish featuring a variety of stir-fried vegetables in a clear and jammy sauce, topped with chopped chives, suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the grated carrots and cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the sliced zucchini and bell pepper, and cook until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
In a small bowl, whisk together the vegetable broth and cornstarch mixture, and pour it over the vegetables.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Season the stir-fry with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the stir-fry to a serving platter and garnish with chopped chives.
